title: cistus × hybridus subsp. grandiflorus
intro: Cistus × hybridus subsp. grandiflorus, also known as the large-flowered rockrose, is a hybrid plant species within the Cistus genus, characterized by its large, showy flowers and shrub-like habit. The medicinal properties of this plant include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Cistus × hybridus subsp. grandiflorus include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, salves, and tinctures, which are often used topically to treat skin conditions, wounds, and other inflammatory issues.
